\n\n“‘Higher calling’ is a good way of putting it, I think,” she says, taking another drink out of her own glass. “I named my son Vernon to remind me of how I got here in the first place, and what I’d be using my skills for going forward.” Lorelei winks at you. “You’d never guess how liberating it feels to be doing something for a greater good, even if it’s something as base as thievery. After all, what good is a pretty face if you have an ugly heart?”
\n\nYou toast to that, and refill your drinks.
\n\nYou do have one last question, though. Why all the gems and jewels? She clearly isn’t pawning them.
\n\n“Those are for momma,” she says. “Like I told you, all the universe’s most beautiful things belong with me.”
Her eyes scan the collar you’re wearing. “All of them.”
\n\nYou eye the photo of her and her son and put that statement to the image of her adoption agency. It might be best not to think about it too hard.

You ask Lorelei, if it isn’t too forward of you to, about her suit. You’ve seen her do all kinds of tricks with it: it clung to her like it was taut rubber, but it breathed like it was silk. She could channel electricity through her hands with it.
And, how could you forget, it parted on command when she ‘inaugurated’ you as her possession.
You’re unfamiliar with whatever technology it’s using.
\n\n“That’s because it isn’t technology,” she says, grinning like a boy with a dirty secret to tell.

“It’s a type of amorphous, inorganic, multicellular symbiote created by a deity known as Gnull, to assist him in destroying other higher-beings known as Stellars. All symbiotes, like my own, bond to their host and amplify their physiques and characteristics, turning them into something they wouldn’t be capable of otherwise.”

“It’s a type of semi-sentient tar that had been enchanted by the indigenous Naleen tribes of Mhen’ga. By cutting out the heart of a virgin on the first blue moon of the year and submerging it into the tar, it takes on the heart’s memories and personalities, and it can be taught to bond with a host and be subject to their every whim and desire.”

Like before, the tendrils slip down into her cleavage – but, evidently feeling frisky, she has the material collect around each breast, boosting their size from her already-enviable D-cups straight up to E’s. \n\nYou ask her how she ‘communicates’ with the dress. She mentioned that she’s connected to it ‘neurologically.’ Does she mean, like... on an intellectual level, or something?

When I want less material, it all goes right into this little receiver, and when I want more, this is where it comes from.” She turns back toward you, a downright giddy smile on her face. “You see in those old-timey classic movies, about thieves breaking into banks or museums, and they’re wearing belts and heavy backpacks full of all the tools they need... I can’t imagine working like that.”

You ask Lorelei what brings her to Zhèng Shi. She’s a woman of finer tastes and standards; Zhèng Shi is hot, dirty, stuffy, and is filled to the brim with a bunch of deplorables that, if they had their way, wouldn’t hesitate to debase her to their level. And yet, she’s not only stayed her long enough to fully furnish her own little pocket of paradise, but she has a loyal doorman. What’s her history here?

\n\nLorelei has her wine to her mouth by the time you finish speaking, and take a moment to breathe before answering. “Goodness,” she says, “that’s so many questions all at once, darling.” She smiles that sweet, charming, disarming smile of hers. “I don’t mind answering, of course. I’d want to know all about me, too. I am fascinating, after all.”

\n\nYou share a laugh at that, and you wait respectfully for her to pick a question and start answering. “Well,” she starts, sliding her finger along the rim of her wine glass, “The truth is, I’m actually here on hired business. Care to guess by whom?”

\n\nA mining company, like RhenWorld, Pyrite Heavy Industries, or even Steele Tech? The base has a mining operation; surely one of them would like to stake their claim to something as mineral-rich as Zhèng Shi.
\n\n“An interesting, and well-reasoned guess, darling, but no, I was not hired by any mining company.”
\n\nPerhaps a rival ne’er-do-well gang, looking to try and encroach on the Zhèng Shi pirate’s turf? Like, the Black Void, maybe?

\n\nLorelei frowns at the suggestion. “The Black Void are thugs, brutes, and murderers. They operate too differently from how I do, and besides, they’re beneath me.” However, she winks at you. “You’re getting closer, though.”

\n\nYou rack your brain for a moment, trying to think of anyone else that might want to take advantage of Lorelei’s skillset. Exasperated, you suggest she’s working for the Zhèng Shi pirates themselves, knowing that it would make no sense. Why would she be hired to steal from her own employer’s people?

\n\nLorelei laughs at the suggestion. “It would be a unique twist, wouldn’t it? Maybe someone up in the chain of command is trying to keep their team on their toes! But no, that’s a little too fantastic of a suggestion, I’m afraid.”
\n\nYou tell her that you give up.
\n\n“I’m here on behalf of the United Galactic Confederacy.”
\n\nYou’re surprised – you’d have thought she’d want to stay as far away from the law as she could. You ask how they even got into contact with her.

\n\nA couple of suggestions immediately come to mind, but you reason away all of them – whoever hired Lorelei must have quite a bit of power and resources to not only contact her, but convince her in the first place. Off the top of your head, that leaves only two major possibilities: Nova Securities, and the United Galactic Confederacy.

\n\nIf it’s one of those two, you ask yourself: what would either of them want with Zhèng Shi? You figure that Nova Securities would be on neutral terms with the pirate base, if anything, so if she were hired by them, it’d be to keep tabs on the base, nothing more.

\n\nWhereas the U.G.C., being the governing body of most of the known universe, would have every incentive to want to crack down on something as illicit as a pirate base in an asteroid, performing illegal mining operations and running a slave trade. Lorelei would naturally want to stay away from the law – but, at the same time, the law could be a very powerful ally for a criminal.
\n\nStill, all this inner monologuing does nothing to humor Lorelei
, which is why you’re here in the first place
. You decide to make your guess: she’s here on behalf of the United Galactic Confederacy.

\n\nLorelei blinks in surprise. “Uh, yes,” she says haltingly. “That’s... that’s correct.” Her eyes fall from yours to the floor, totally unsure of how she should process your completely correct guess. “Wow.”
\n\nYou ask the first obvious question that comes to mind: how did they even come into contact with her?
\n\n“That’s actually a whole story for another time, I’m afraid,” she says. “What’s important is that they did, and we... worked out a deal.”

a pet like

Then, she swallows. “They offered me two of the things I coveted the most; and they only wanted one.” She winks at you, her teeth snidely poking from her curling lips. “I give them information, and they give me amnesty... and all the jewels a lady could want.”
\n\nWhy would the pirates of Zhèng Shi give her the information she’s after? Hell, why would they give her a private room, allow her to decorate it, and let her hire a doorman?

\n\nShe hums, staring into her wine glass on the counter as she considers her response to your question. “Tell me, darling... if information is the most powerful weapon in an arsenal, what weapon is potent enough to destabilize it?”
\n\nYou think on it, but you don’t know. Information isn’t physical, and once it’s obtained, it can’t be destroyed. You don’t know what could be more powerful than that.
\n\n“Misinformation, honey.”
\n\nIf information is so highly coveted because of its potency, then the only thing that could beat it is sabotage. Misinformation. Lies.

Very good.

\n\n“Ask anyone on Zhèng Shi why I’m here, and you’ll get anything from me being hired help from Nova Securities, sent to test their patrols and their systems from exploits; to me being one of Maike’s personal concubines; to me being a fencer for their mining operations, selling the jewels for credits and helping them fund their plans....”

\n\nShe looks around the room, to all the gems she has studded into the walls and floors. “Maybe not so much that last one,” she laughs. “But if there’s any one thing any of them will tell you, it’s that I am not to be questioned.”

Why Here?

Knowing her line of work, Lorelei must be well acquainted with the rest of the peoples of Zhèng Shi (whether they want her to or not). You ask her what her opinion is on the groups and factions wandering the asteroid with her.

\n\nShe lowers her wine glass from her mouth and frowns in thought at the question. “Pirates,” she whispers to herself disdainfully. You can tell that she doesn’t like thinking about most of them. “Brutes; thugs; deviants; the lot of them.” She sets down her wine glass and grasps your hand, looking you in the eye. “I’m sure this will be rich to hear, but there’s a certain finesse to theft as a craft. If thievery were like baking, I would consider myself a world-class artiste, and I make the finest cakes, fit for royalty, and those pirates are little more than meat butchers.”
\n\nYou take it to mean that she isn’t fond of any of them, and that she’d prefer to interact with them as little as possible.

\n\n“It’s difficult to find a diamond in the rough in a place as coarse as Zhèng Shi, darling, and I am neither vain, nor,” she continues, raising her wine glass and toasting it to you, “drunk enough to exclude myself.” She takes a swig – a full, hearty one, unlike the ladylike sips she had been taking up to that point. “I can at least consider myself to be a finely-sanded pebble compared to their droppings-covered-in-cat-litter.”

\n\nShe clearly doesn’t hold them in a high regard, but, perhaps, you can work with that; it’ll at least give her something to talk about, and Lorelei isn’t one to pass up the opportunity to talk about things. The only ‘problem’ I have with them is their... penchant for jewels and gems, and I’m not overly fond of competition, but so long as no jewel leaves this room, I can learn to coexist with them.”

“But that’s about all the leniency I have for the fools in this cesspit.” She points one finger at you, almost accusatorily. “The Laquine Jumpers? Ditzy, aimless, rutting beasts, the lot of them. They each have endowments so large, it’s a wonder they have the focus to do any piracy, much less the function to press themselves into those skintight suits of theirs.”

\n\nShe extends a second finger towards you. “The Slyveren ‘Slavebreakers,’ they call them? They’re unprofessional; they lack drive; and, worst of all, they cheat! \n\n“And those... ‘Sex Op’ Kaithrits, or whatever they call themselves,” she continues, extending a third finger. “They are somehow simultaneously the best of the bunch, and the worst. They put on the facade of having discipline, and they’re charged with keeping ‘the peace’ on Zhèng Shi, but their constitution is as weak as their superior’s. I could consider forgiving a band of mercenaries just doing their job, but flash them a few times and they’ll be too lustful to even do them! If there’s any one thing that isn’t sexy, [pc.name], it’s a lack of discipline.”

\n\nShe raises her wine glass to her lips, but, just as her lips touch the glass, she hesitates and reconsiders. She had been pounding down the wine as she went; maybe, in the heat of the moment, she had realized how intense she was being and decided to hold back.

\n\n“But the absolute worst of them all are the management.” She lowers her glass and traces the rim with her finger, making the wine glass sing a low, wailing note for you both. “Maike, the Overseer, whom I’m sure you’ve met by now, and two of her cohorts: one Laquine they call the Shock Hopper, and another Laquine they call,” she groans, “‘Large Dick Collider.’” She forces out every word in the Laquine’s name, struggling with them like they were rubber in her mouth. “To this day, still easily the most ridiculous name I’ve ever heard.”
\n\nLorelei’s eyes meet yours, and you sense an unusual serenity within them: her emotions about the management in Zhèng Shi are pure and unfiltered, for better or worse.

\n\n“It has nothing to do with their work ethic. They run the asteroid with an iron fist, and for good reason: nobody else is as qualified to run this place as they are. It has to do with their slave trade.”
\n\nShe pauses and tilts her head down, her lips curling into themselves again and again, as she considers how she wants to phrase what she’s about to say next.

\n\n“Credits and gems are one thing. Equipment is one thing. The parts to build an enormous spaceship... that’s one thing.” Without lifting her head, her eyes lock back onto yours, with a steely determination that wasn’t there before. “This may be because I’m a single mother running an orphanage, but stealing a life and making them your slave isn’t something I can look past. They’re the worst, most vile creatures in this universe, [pc.name], and the sooner Zhèng Shi is dismantled, the better.”
\n\nYou ask her if that’s a part of her personal agenda.
\n\n“With any luck.”
\n\nIt’s safe to say that she’s done with this conversation.

blockHeader("Leaving")
You get up to excuse yourself. It was nice meeting Lorelei in her little pocket of paradise in Zhèng Shi, but unfortunately, duty calls.

\n\nLorelei hums, then smiles sweetly. “I understand,” she says, one hand gently trailing down the side of your face, cupping your cheek. “We both have our vices and our responsibilities that we must abide by.” She blows you a kiss. “Don’t leave your mistress waiting too long, [pc.name]. I have a bar and a bed that’s in desperate need of warming nowadays.”

\n\nBefore you leave, you take your wine glass, and Lorelei’s, and wash and dry them in the sink she has installed behind the countertop, then replace them and the bottle of wine just where you found them. She watches you with amused patience – it seems to earn you points with her. She smiles as you leave, and her smile is the last thing on your mind as you shut the door to her room behind you.

\n\n“I understand,” Lorelei says, before blowing you a kiss. “I’m sure someone of your upbringing is quite the outgoing entrepreneur. Get out there and make your mark on this station for us both, darling.”

\n\nYou do Lorelei the courtesy of placing your wine glass in the sink behind the countertop and re-corking and replacing the bottle before you show yourself out. Lorelei sees you off with a smile – and her smile is the last thing you see before you shut the door behind you.

Lorelei’s eyes widen in disbelief, that you would show yourself into her personal quarters, then dirty her glasses with her fine, expensive wine, and then try to leave without so much as asking how her day was, or without sampling the wine, or even complimenting her on her dress. \n\nFor the first time, she steps around you, towards your front. It’s too dark for you to make out her details, however, and, not helping things, her suit is pitch black all over. The most you can make out is her silhouette: she’s a shapely woman, with a matronly hourglass figure, but you can’t make out her sizes or her facial features in this light.
\n\n<i>“And I know you. I know you better than I’m sure you’d find comfortable, "+pc.mf("handsome","beautiful")+". You’re not a slave
, and that getup of yours isn’t fooling me for a moment
. But, all of our meetings together,” she sighs, drawing one gloved hand up the side of her thigh slowly, “you’ve proven that you’re simply... too easy to take advantage of.”

\n\nShe bends at the waist to reach the same hand towards you. It draws a lazy, gentle circle around your jaw, before coming to a rest just beneath your chin. “So, I decided... if you’re no pirate, and you’re on Zhèng Shi, then I might as well make you my slave. Why bother taking your credits, darling, when I can just snatch you up, as my greatest prize?”

\n\nThe words leave her mouth as a stutter; she’s so excited by the idea that she can barely contain it. She continues, her hand coming back up and rubbing her fingers languidly through your [pc.hair]. “Think about it. You wouldn’t have to worry about any of the other undesirables on the base trying to take you for their own if they know you’re owned property. And, best of all,” she hisses, her voice quaking in barely-contained excitement at the thought, “you’d belong to me. Like all the universe’s most beautiful things should.”

\n\nYou note that, from the way she’s talking, she’s making it sound like you have a choice in the matter. “Oh, of course you do, darling,” she assures you, rubbing your scalp like you were a kid and you just said something silly. “I could slap one of that Urbolg’s collars on you and call it a day, I could. But for you to give me control like that would mean that I’ve stolen your heart – and, any woman would tell you, there is nothing more valuable in this world than that.”

\n\n“Come, [pc.name],” Minuet says, her palm coming down to rest against your cheek, caressing it as if she were about to lean in and give you your first kiss. “Enough with the games. No more threats; no more stealing. You’ve never put up a fight before. Why start now?” She leans over you, her breath washing against your ear; her tongue snakes out and gives your earlobe a thorough, but teasingly-quick, pass. “If you’re a good [pc.boyGirl] and give me what I want without a fuss, I’m willing to... shall we say, celebrate your inauguration. To make things more enticing.”

\n\nMinuet’s words ring heavily in your ears. You’re in some forgotten service hallway – if it’s even that long, it could very well be some kind of closet – on your [pc.knees], facing a woman you’ve met, but never seen, before, and she’s asking you to wilfully become her slave. And she’s promising you a very good time if you accept.
\n\nDo you?
Yes
No
You make your way deeper into Zhèng Shi–
\n\n“Don’t move.”
\n\nBefore you know it, your right arm is pinned behind your back, held in place by a woman that had moved silent as the night. Something sharp and thin presses insistently against your neck.

\n\n“The legendary [pc.name] Steele, in the flesh,” she purrs, her warm breath caressing your bare neck, where her needle meets your [pc.skinFurScales]. Despite the strength going into keeping you in place, the hand gripping your wrist feels rather soft, like she’s wearing silk gloves. “
As dark and serious as your cousin.
My, darling, but what brings you to this rank pit? Don’t tell me someone of your stature has thrown their lot in with some petty thieves.”

\n\nA few responses come to mind, but every time you think you’re about to say something, her needle keeps you in check. Noting the risk, you take the less-risky option and ask whom you’re speaking with.
\n\n“You’ll forgive me if we’re not quite on a first-name basis yet, cutie,” she teases. “You can call me Minuet for now. Just for now, hun.”

\n\nYou try and flex a bit, to turn and see Minuet, but she keeps you held fast, and her needle grazes against you. “Ah ah,” she trills playfully. “I prefer you where you are, right here.” Seems you’ll need her permission before you can get a look at her.

\n\n“It’s not every day you get to meet the "+pc.mf("heir","heiress")+" to a throne as gilded as Steele Tech’s head position,” she says. You feel her body press into you, and her chin resting against the thick of your shoulder, as she appreciates you and your form. This visit may be as much pleasure for her as it is business. “
You came here for daddy’s probe, right? Heaven knows why you’ve stuck around.
You’re here for daddy’s – what, third, fourth probe, if I’m right?
Ah, but you’ll forgive me if we skip the pleasantries.”
\n\nYou ask her what
the fuck
she wants. “Oooh, already so... willing,” she says. “We can work with that later, darling, if you’re interested. But for now, see, I think I’d rather help you.”
\n\nYou don’t like the sound of that, but you’re not in a position to argue.

\n\n“Mmmm,” she hums as she leans back. You can feel her eyes roam across your body, towards your waist. “Credit chits are unwieldy, cumbersome things, aren’t they?” she asks, her voice light, like she’s making schoolyard conversation. “Why don’t you let me take them off your hands, darling? They must weigh so much on you.”

\n\nShe rummages around behind you some more, taking inventory of your belongings. “Goodness,” you hear her mutter to herself. “And you’re carrying some awfully pretty stones around. Pretty things like that belong to a pretty girl like me, don’t you think?”

\n\nYou tell her that if she looks half as ugly as her personality, she should consider it a blessing. “Ooh, feisty,” she hisses. The pinch of her needle presses into your [pc.skinFurScales]. Maybe you should shut up. “I’ll be sure to keep that temper of yours in mind next time, darling.”
\n\nMinuet has you in a pretty poor spot: her grip is tight on your hand, and even if you had the strength to break free
(and you’d be surprised if you didn’t)
, she could gut your neck before you could do any damage to her. One slip of her fingers, and you’d start bleeding out on the spot.
\n\nDo you submit to her demands?

\n\nOnce again, your arm is pinned behind you – you’re starting to get awfully familiar with the way Minuet is introducing herself to you. You initially struggle, but then relax, when you feel her grip on your wrist and the way her body softly presses against your backside. She even takes her time bringing her needle up to your neck, as if it were a formality rather than a threat.

\n\n“Hello again, "+pc.mf("handsome","beautiful")+",” Minuet purrs. You don’t strain against her, and although her grip on your wrist is just as strong as ever, she’s only putting in as much effort to keep you restrained as you are to try and break free. “What’s a good-looking honey like yourself doing in a dump like this?”

\n\nYou note that you two are getting awfully comfortable with each other, as of late. “And it’s terribly unprofessional of me, I know,” she sighs, “but you’ve just been such a wonderful target, [pc.name] Steele. I oh-so love it when people just give me what I want without having to make things difficult, and, given our business record, so do you.”

\n\nShe leans into your body once more, pressing her own softly against yours. “I don’t normally do this, honey, but I’ve been thinking of maybe... giving you a ‘loyalty reward.’ For being such an easy target all this time.” Boldly, she rests her cheek against your shoulder and dips her needle away, kissing the spot it had just been practiced on. “You’ve been such a fountain of fortune for me – the least I could do is share the fun a bit, don’t you think?”

\n\nYou tell her that she would definitely be one of the ritzier whores you’ve ever spent your money on. “Hmm,” she hums, pulling away from you, “they certainly didn’t bother to take that silver spoon out of your mouth so they could teach you some manners.” She pulls back in, dropping her voice to a whisper. “But that’s alright,” she purrs, “I’ve broken my share of "+pc.mf("bulls","cows")+" before. I could show you, if you’re not willing to cooperate this time.”

\n\nYou wonder if there isn’t an easier way to do this – one where you both have a ‘good time,’ without it costing you a huge chunk of your cash. “Mmm, I’m afraid I’m not quite that generous, darling. Nothing good in this universe is for free.” She leans back and clicks her tongue as she eyes your pack. “But I can promise that it’ll be ever-so worth it.”
\n\nAs before, Minuet is demanding your credits
, and she’s probably after one of your gems as well
. Do you submit to her extortion and give her what she wants?
\n\n“Don’t move.”

\n\nOnce again, your hand is locked behind your back, and you feel the slight sting of a needle and its point pressing against your neck. Minuet has sprung her trap on you once again, and you’re at her mercy.

\n\n“Fancy seeing you again, darling,” she muses. Last time, she handled you rather roughly, though this time, while her grip is just as strong, she’s rather lax on her treatment on you. “Still on your best behavior, I trust?”

\n\nYou respond that you wouldn’t dream of acting out, with her and her needlepoint as they are. “Good [pc.boyGirl],” she purrs, her body pressing gently against your back. “Been keeping the bad guys at bay?”
\n\nWell... clearly not.

\n\n“I’m hurt!” she pouts. “I’m as angelic as they come, darling. You just haven’t gotten to know the good side of me.” She laughs to herself. “Or... any side, now that I think about it.” Her lips pull in, against your ear, and she licks her lips; she’s close enough to you that you can hear her tongue run over them. “We don’t have to be enemies, you know. Maybe I can show you a friendlier side of me someday.” Her voice drops to a whisper; you feel her hot breath against your ear as she mouths, “maybe more than just one side.”

\n\nShe pulls back, resuming her regular posture. “But that’s a conversation for a less... volatile time, darling. You know what I’m after. Are you going to be a nice [pc.boyGirl] like last time and not give me a fuss about it?”
\n\nLike last time, Minuet wants your credits
, and, probably, one of your gems
. Do you give them to her?
Yes
No

It’s almost enough to make you dizzy. “Ah,” Lorelei says when she sees you. She’s wearing the same black dress with high heels as the first time you had met her here. “I was just thinking of you, [pc.name]. Saurmorian Doorman

As you explore and familiarize yourself with the Recreational Deck, you come to what appears to be a doorman of sorts: a large, imposing Saurmorian, wearing mostly leather with metallic inseams, protecting the entrance to a room. You’ve noticed that there aren’t a lot of individual rooms in the recreational deck, so whatever’s in there must be worth protecting. He doesn’t carry himself like the rest of the riffraff about the deck, and in fact, most others wandering the place seem to try and avoid him.
\n\nThe Saurmorian gets a good look at you, too, and smiles to himself as soon as he sees the collar around your neck.

\n\n“You must be the ‘darling,’” the doorman says, beckoning to you with his finger to approach. “Minuet spoke highly of you. She’s been expecting you.” He steps to one side and allows you to enter the door. “Consider yourself lucky.”
\n\nBefore you take his invitation, you ask the doorman what to expect.

\n\n“Dunno. I’ve never had the... pleasure of having a one-on-one sitdown with her. She just pays me to watch the door, so I watch the door.” The Saurmorian sizes you up, likely comparing you to someone or something – perhaps himself. “She’s damn fine, though, and I wouldn’t mind wearing a collar if it meant I got a piece of her.”
\n\nYou suppose you’ll have to see what Minuet is like through closed doors yourself.
\n\nYou tell the Saurmorian that you don’t know how good of a lay she is, but is she’s half as good as she tastes, you’ll be in for a wild night. He grins, and extends his fist for you to bump.
You show yourself into Minuet’s room, closing the door behind you.

\n\nYou try and peer over the Saurmorian’s shoulder and see if there’s a nameplate or something on the door, to try and glean what it is he’s protecting, but he steps up to you – not in an imposing manner, but specifically to keep you from trying to look past him.
\n\n“This is a private room,” he says, answering your unspoken question. “And the lady of the house is away on business. Get lost.”
\n\n‘The lady of the house’? Sounds like someone you’ll want to be on their good side – or someone you should avoid entirely. You take the Saurmorian’s advice and turn around.
Next
\n\nThe Saurmorian eyes you up and down, getting a good look at you.

\n\n“You [pc.name]?” he asks, and you respond affirmatively. “Minuet told me to expect you. Said to let you in once you made it to the deck.” He steps aside, allowing you past him and to the door. “She spoke highly of you. It’s not often anyone on this rock impresses her.”

\n\nWithout going into specifics, you recall how you met her, and the whole ordeal it was to earn your ‘good graces’ with her. She’s certainly among the more capable of anyone you’ve met on Zhèng Shi.

\n\n“You’ll have to teach me how you did it,” the Saurmorian says, making small-talk with you. “I’ve never had the pleasure of having a one-on-one sitdown with her. She pays me to watch the door, so I watch the door.” He sizes you up, likely comparing you to someone or something – perhaps himself. A grin spreads across his face as he fantasizes about a ‘personal session’ with her. “I’d wear her like a belt if I ever had the opportunity.”
\n\nYou keep any further comments to yourself as you pass by the doorman and enter Minuet’s room.

You tell the Saurmorian that you can’t be sure who’s going to be wearing who by the end of tonight - Minuet doesn’t seem like the type to bend to someone very easily. But you’re also not about to complain.

The Saurmorian extends his fist for you to bump as he lets you enter.
Next

\n\nIt’s like stepping into a completely different world: the Recreational Deck is a dirty, lawless place, but Lorelei’s room is extravagant, opulent, personal, and highly intimate. It’s almost enough to make you dizzy.

\n\n“Ah,” Lorelei says when she sees you. She’s wearing the same black dress with high heels as the first time you had met her here. “I was just thinking of you, [pc.name].".concat(lorelei.isHerBitch()?"It gets so lonely sometimes, knowing that my collection remains incomplete when you’re out and about.":"It’s always good to see you again. All the gems in the universe can’t compare to company like yours.","”")
\n\nFamiliar with her wants
you retrieve Lorelei’s collar from your effects and slip it around your neck; then

, you take your position behind the bar and pull out two wine glasses, followed by a bottle from her personal rack. Her eyes are on you; when you withdraw a bottle, she smiles in both acknowledgment and appreciation, and says nothing when you pour the wine into the glasses.
\n\n“Come,” she says
, motioning to the empty stool next to her. “Sit with me. Talk with me.”
, her inflection almost-but-not-quite making it sound like a command. “Entertain me.”
\n\nYou take your seat next to her. What do you do next?
Aha, you’re back at the big dig pit. Exactly what you’re doing here is your own guess but you can’t help but dwell on some vague, bubbly reason you can’t qui-
\n\nTHNK, THNKK
\n\nOh crap.
\n\nTwo metal rods crash into the ground at your feet and the all-too-familiar flutter of insectoid wings drones closer and closer!
\n\nFeeling the deja vu kick in, you instinctively back away from the impact zone
and ready your [pc.weapon]. You’ve got a new way of getting around her now
. The Overseer lands as dramatically as her first entrance and wastes no time in reeling her grapples in, cycling the ammo with a flick of the wrist and firing a hefty bolas shot towards your middle!
\n\nAt such short range, there’s no way it can miss and the energy-threaded weights swing around your middle.
Next

Out from the twisting gloom of the mineshafts and twinkling minerals, a grand pit comes into your view. ‘Grand’ doesn’t quite do it justice: it must be at least 60 feet in diameter but what takes you back is the depth. "+(isSillyModeEnabled()?"It’s even deeper than all the fanfiction in the world.":"Starting from the walkway you’re planted on, a wide path twists around the inside of the pit and presumably to the bottom.")

\n\nThere’s enough nearby artificial lighting to help you make out where to go: bluish white lamps and lights that cover the walkway and freshly chartered ore veins show you where you would go... but not where you’d go to.

\n\nThere’s a constant tnk-tnk coming from below, a whole chorus of metal-on-rock that resonates upwards. You can barely make out hunched over slaves and machines hacking and grinding away at the rock along the lower pathways. With a cautious [pc.walk], you get close to the edge of the walkway and peer into the near-black below...
\n\nYep, that’s deep.
\n\nPretty deep.
\n\nDeep enough that the lights are merely dots on the grayish ground below.

\n\nNerves get the better of you and you can’t help but shuffle backwards to the wall. The footpath is comfortably wide, perhaps a good ten-or-so feet. Enough for a chain gang... and with a huff through the nose, you get back to examining the rest of this cavernous pit.
\n\nYour eyes are drawn to the lighting fixtures and you’re a little taken aback by just how high up they are. Must be at least as high as the pit is wide!
\n\nOne of the lights shines over your [pc.leg] and is unerringly hot against your [pc.skinFurScales].
\n\nYour codex beeps with an alert: perhaps it’s best not to stand under them for long. No wonder they’re up so high...
\n\nYou’re about to move out of the light when-
\n\nTNGG, TNNGGG
Next
\n\nMaike’s remote is on the ground where you left it. You could still free the slaves, if you wished.
Release Slaves
\n\nSilence reigns below. The crowds of slaves have moved on. With a cautious [pc.walk], you get close to the edge of the walkway and peer into the near-black below...
\n\nYep, that’s deep.
\n\nPretty deep.
\n\nDeep enough that the lights are merely dots on the grayish ground below.

\n\nNerves get the better of you and you can’t help but shuffle backwards to the wall. The footpath is comfortably wide, perhaps a good ten-or-so feet. Enough for a chain gang... and with a huff through the nose, you get back to examining the rest of this cavernous pit.

Presumably "+(1===flags.TIVF_MAIKE_TALK?"Overseer Maike":"one of the head slavers in the mine")+", this towering insectoid woman has you pinned to the ground.

\n\nShe stands at 6' 7\" with a graceful, subtly sexy posture. "+(pc.tallness>79?" If you were side-by-side, her height wouldn’t mean so much but since you’re on the ground...":"")+" What adds to this is the sheer size of her moth-like wings. Six thick wings flicker and waft in the warm air of the mine, bathed in a gentle orange-red glow as light passes through the patterned, translucent membranes. What’s left creates an ominous shadow that covers your prone form.

\n\nThe "+(1===flags.TIVF_MAIKE_TALK?"Overseer’s":"slaver’s")+" body could only be described as athletic and wiry, an overt litheness to her limbs belying her raw physical strength and dexterity. She was able to plummet from heights unseen and handily knock you to the ground after all. "+(pc.PQ()>=80?"Even with your strength, she took you to town. ":"")+"That said, she is clearly a bombshell: a toned hourglass shape with curvy hips, a bouncy, grabbable ass and a soft set of healthy, bare E-cup breasts on full display. An array of symmetrical silver piercings make artwork of her near-flat nipples, reflecting the light to become dozens of tiny twinkling dots in the darkness.

\n\nWhat takes away from her attention to the aesthetic are the chitinous plates that cover her insectoid frame. The sides of her hips, legs and arms are covered in bony, pale-green plates that glisten under the lights. Said plates are intermingled with softer, smooth-looking scales that blend from pale-green to purple and then to a sensuous blue. You think that there’s some serious mod-work going on: these plates look unnaturally strong. Her feet and hands could be interchanged if not for details: five digits a-piece with the appendages crested with more scales, tapering off into vicious claws. With one splayed foot planted flat against you, you don’t need to guess at its manual dexterity.

\n\nA long and thick tail swings and slithers around the air like a spectral snake. It’s easily the length of the slaver herself and the tip is covered in four pale-green plates that jitter and flap with some unknown intent. What could be hidden within there is anyone’s guess...

\n\nHer face is exceedingly feminine but still more alien than human. She has unusually straight and sharp features with slanted, blazing orange eyes. Those eyes bore into you with their 3-slit pupils, attempting to steal your attention in these brief moments. A tight bundle of long, silver quills hangs over her shoulder like something akin to hair but made of something more rigid. It’s all held in place by two long, elfin ears that sweep back much like her horns. Around her neck is a natural ‘scarf’ of downy gray fur, one that conforms to a V shape towards her cleavage and adds another layer of curious sophistication. Two sleek horns of greenish-white curve back from her forehead and add a devilish spin, another alien twist to her evolutionary biology.

\n\nShe wears a complex suit of armor, although ‘armor’ could be stretching the word. An ornate corset of silver and black covers her hourglass middle, giving some minimal support for her exposed bust. The corset fails to cover much else, leaving a thong-like strip below the waist to cover the slaver’s nethers. Flexible, silk-like fabric connects it to her shoulder armor, which itself is quite bulky, almost spherical and made of a similar silver metal to the corset. You wonder what those strange shoulder-parts could be for. On her wrists are rather fetching cuffs that are connected to the shoulders by more loose fabric. The only armor below the hips comes in the form of two silver repulsors that fit seamlessly to the back of her calves. Explains how she was able to fly and land so swiftly and easily...
\n\nAround her waist is a wide belt with items of interest: card keys in holsters, a gray rod with some buttons on it, a strange hand cannon with two barrels, some satchel of bulging square objects.
\n\n“Answer me!”
\n\nAnother whip-strike cracks by your ear.
\n\nUhh, uhhh!
Appearance
Let’s hope this disguise works!
\n\n“I’m just a slave. I got lost! I couldn’t see where I was going!” you blurt out as you try to wriggle into a more comfortable breathing position. Damn claws are pressing on your chest!

\n\n“A wriggler, like... bait on a hook. Oo, should I, shouldn’t I?” she says out loud, seeming to disregard your answer. Her clawed foot slides ever closer to your exposed neck while her tail snakes above your gaze. The smooth, pale-green plates jitter and flap like perverse petals caught in unseen winds, and you can’t help but jerk your head from side to side as it ‘nips’ at your cheek and chin.

\n\nSuddenly a new tightness grips you by the throat! You panic and grasp at the force on instinct. "+(isSillyModeEnabled()?"Oh no, your most vital area! ":"")+"It has to be her foot, and sure enough, you quickly focus on the muscled thigh that very slowly begins lifting you up off of the ground. "+(pc.tallness>=84?"Must be the work of those repulsors, especially with you being on the weighty side! ":"")+"You try to punch away, smack or knock the limb away but your hands just knock against unfeeling chitin and hardened scales.

\n\n“Where... is your collar?” comes a question so clear in malice that it blots out even the noise of the mining from below. The grip around your neck loosens just enough for you to blurt out an answer...
It Broke
Unprocessed
Pen
Fuck You
“It broke! I-I got lost looking for engineering! I really wanted to put it on but it wouldn’t fit!” you force out in a high pitched voice, the strain on your throat relaxing as you sputter.

\n\n“Damnable Urbolg, him and his... ‘prototypes’,” she growls, a deep and animalistic secondary tone vibrating through her body like a true apex creature. “And yet all of your middling brain matter led you here. How am I to ease my doubt of your claim, slave?”
\n\nThe grip tightens once again and you whine as your [pc.face] flushes with heat.

\n\n“Oh, is that hard enough for you, mmm? I can tell you’re new if you’re giving in that easily,” she mocks with a sardonic grin. She even pulls a faux-pout and shakes her head at you. “Mwaaah, the big bad Overseer is putting me in my place, waaah.”
\n\nYou try to kick and flail at her other leg but hit only the bony plates covering the sides. Your efforts are met with the most indulgent and devilish of giggles.
\n\n“Yesss, there’s fire in you! And here I was thinking to throw you off of the walkway here and now,” she laughs and you’re promptly thrown back to the ground.
\n\nYou’re barely given time to breathe as you’re hoisted up to a wobbly standing stance by the scruff of your neck.

\n\n“You’re going to go back to where you came and then up to the main elevator. I’ll know if you deviate. There will be nowhere that I can’t find you, nowhere for you to hide and shirk your reason for living,” she growls from behind with her grasp pulling you down by the back of your head. “You will learn that Overseer Maike owns you.”

\n\nWith that, her hand swiftly darts to your neck with some sort of semi-circle in its grip. Maike slams it against your throat and at once, a tight metallic ring snaps around your neck! The laughing slaver pushes you away from her and you stagger forwards on your shaky [pc.footOrFeet].
\n\n“Hurry hurry! If you stay on course, the mining bots won’t find you, ahahaha!”

\n\nThe collar beeps and blurts out something in a synthetic voice: “Signal Received. Collar Configured. Owner: Excavator Overseer Maike Erisu. Addendum: We Hope You Enjoy Your Stay At Zhèng Shi Mines! We’re All a Part of The Plan!”
\n\nYour breath catches as the cold metal fits to your neck and you can’t help but clutch at it in panic and in vain.
\n\n“Oh don’t be like that, you’ll love it here! I’ll make sure of it. Or at least, the collar will,” is what the slave-driver says in her most disarming of voices.

\n\nYour hands touch the metal ring all over to get a feel for it: not tight enough to choke but impossible to ignore. You can barely turn your head and if you did, the cold metal snags at your [pc.skinFurScales] sharply enough to discourage it. You prod a little more but hrrrkk-

\n\nYour throat closes up. Your body stiffens all over. Everything below your neck roils with flesh-sundering pain. You’re on your [pc.knees] before you even know it as if it were a throbbing, overwhelming vertigo.
